title: National - The Kathmandu Post
authors: By Krishna Prasad Gautam & Shiva Puri
By Ghana Shyam Gautam & Parbat Portel
summary: National US assistant secretary Kapur arrives in Nepal By Post Report Top US official lands in Kathmandu on Monday. He is scheduled to meet ministers and business leaders during three-day visit. Sanjeev Satgainya appointed as Editor, Features & Investigations for Kantipur Publications By Post Report Satgainya, who brings more than two decades of experience in Nepal’s English-language journalism to
content:
National US assistant secretary Kapur arrives in Nepal By Post Report Top US official lands in Kathmandu on Monday. He is scheduled to meet ministers and business leaders during three-day visit. Sanjeev Satgainya appointed as Editor, Features & Investigations for Kantipur Publications By Post Report Satgainya, who brings more than two decades of experience in Nepal’s English-language journalism to this position, will lead an independent team of senior reporters and investigative journalists in his new role. Woman-police scuffle video goes viral: what really happened in Sunsari eviction? By Arjun Subedi Footage showing police holding a woman by the neck during a demolition drive raises questions over what led to the confrontation. Seven bodies from September 9 fire remain unidentified as DNA profiling stalls By Gaurav Pokharel Authorities struggle to extract samples from severely burnt remains, delaying identification of the deceased. Birgunj launches drive to demolish over 1,000 roadside structures By Shankar Acharya Local residents and traders protest sudden action as authorities enforce 25-metre road standard along key highway stretch. Bajhang phone users battle patchy network By Basanta Pratap Singh Users complain of weak connectivity, call drops, and one-way audio. Police to enter Nepal colleges to remove student union offices, banners By Sudip Kaini Move aims to dismantle political wings in educational institutions and replace them with student councils. Unions call it ‘undemocratic’. Sindhupalchok steps up forest fire prevention with fire lines in over 30 community forests By Anish Tiwari District forest office accelerates preparedness as rising heat and wind increase wildfire risks; 82 hectares already gutted. Birgunj begins demolition drive on encroached structures By Shankar Acharya Around 1,200 private buildings and 29 government structures fall within a 25-metre road widening plan from Gandak Chowk to Miteri Bridge. BP Highway blocked after rain washes away diversions in Kavre By Jyoti Shrestha Traffic halted since 11pm Saturday after multiple diversion points along the Katunje–Mangaltar stretch were swept away, with vehicles stranded at several locations. Death toll in Salyan jeep crash rises to four By Biplov Maharjan Victims include three who died on the spot; six among the injured in critical condition. Cabinet delay leaves key expressway section in limbo in Khokana By Bimal Khatiwada Proposal to shift toll plaza to Pharsidol awaits Cabinet decision as construction reaches 46.88 percent, Mahadevtar tunnel breakthrough targeted by mid-July. Many local units seek to upgrade. Federal ministry launches process By Anil Giri The federal ministry will start the process as per the provision of revisiting existing set-up before every local elections. Mountain students to suffer more as off Sundays pile on learning holidays By Krishna Prasad Gautam & Shiva Puri Federal directives on enrolment and teaching schedules clash with climatic, social and structural realities across Karnali and Madhesh, causing worries about incomplete courses and worse learning outcomes. ‘Gangster-like behaviour’: Traders clash with Butwal officials over pavement-clearing drive By Ghanashyam Gautam Crackdown on encroachments triggers tensions as traders allege high-handed tactics and poor record-keeping of seized goods. Salyan surgeries halt forces patients to travel outward for care By Biplab Maharjan Closure forces patients to travel as far as Dang, Nepalgunj or Surkhet to seek care—at heavy prices. Sada family struggles to repatriate their son’s body from Malaysia By Bidyananda Ram The Sadas say they do not know the name of the company where their son was working as a security guard. Nepal set for contraceptive shortage amid funding cuts By Arjun Paudel Health ministry has cut the contraceptive budget from Rs250 million to Rs170 million for the new fiscal year, while UNFPA has slashed annual funding from $3.2m to $1.06m. Government moves to probe assets. Questions linger over panel’s mandate and impact By Matrika Dahal & Durga Dulal Ex-justice-led panel will examine wealth of public office holders between 2006 and 2026. Shockwaves of Iran war hit manufacturing, construction sectors in Nepal By Ghana Shyam Gautam & Parbat Portel Impact of skyrocketing petroleum prices and riverbed material shortages threatens factory and project shutdowns.

Unions call it ‘undemocratic’.
